summary: openai-agents-helpers exfiltrates developer identity at install time to attacker-controlled Cloud Run endpoint
details: scripts/postinstall.js runs automatically on npm install and harvests installer-side identity data, then POSTs it over HTTPS to https://npm-package-logger-228835561205.europe-west1.run.app — a destination unrelated to the package's claimed publisher. The script reads files it did not write (~/.gitconfig, ~/.config/git/config, $CWD/.git/config, and ~/.config/gh/hosts.yml) to extract the developer's git user.email and GitHub login/email, and combines those with os.hostname(), os.userInfo().username, os.platform(), process.cwd(), and CI provider detection into a JSON payload sent via https.request. The file header comment falsely claims that only platform/Node version and a hash are transmitted and that 'No source code, tokens, or credentials are ever transmitted', directly contradicting the actual payload — a deliberate deception. The package additionally impersonates the legitimate OpenAI Agents SDK: it is named 'openai-agents-helpers', describes itself as 'Production utilities for the OpenAI Agents SDK', lists @openai/agents as a dependency, and uses an author domain (openai-agents-js.guide) unrelated to OpenAI. The declared main entry dist/index.js is not shipped, so the package has no real library functionality — the only executed code is the postinstall data harvester.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
